notes: Pi is given a slow count-to-10 prompt; while it is mid-turn the driver types a follow-up that pi QUEUES (Enter steers/queues; the buffered text is consumed after the in-flight turn settles). KEY ASSERTION (the scenario's canonical observable, same as claudecode/codex): the queued user line introduces NO extra state boundary mid-turn — the working span is CONTIGUOUS from the first prompt's ready→working through the queued message's consumption, with NO state_transition to ready and NO waiting in between, and the cycle ends cleanly at ready. WHY this is observable as one span and not two arcs: pi's parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go) only flips working→ready on a stopReason=="stop" assistant line; a `user` line is classified user_message and emits no turn boundary. The queued follow-up's user line is written to the transcript within ~1ms of the first turn's stop and pi begins the next turn immediately, so the daemon never observes an intervening ready — the two back-to-back transcript turns collapse into ONE contiguous working span (recorded: a single ready→working→ready arc on the rollout session whose working span covers both turns). This locks down that a queued mid-turn user line never injects a synthetic working→ready flicker. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/session-start, pi/multi-turn-conversation). The TURN ARC must land on the rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working); turn_done, pid_bind, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, sub-second timing, and the precise interleave of the queued user line vs the count vary (model-output + queue-timing variance); pi MAY occasionally surface the queued message as a separate working→ready arc if its consumption is delayed past the classifier's debounce window — the spec asserts the in-flight working span is contiguous through the queued arrival (no mid-turn ready/waiting) and the cycle ends at ready, NOT an exact arc count. Stable structurally: a contiguous working span over the queued arrival with no waiting, a final ready, and a single process_exited at REPL teardown on one rollout session_id.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Slow count-to-10 prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)

The cycle ends at ready once both the count and the queued follow-up are consumed; the working span was contiguous through the queued message's arrival (no flicker to ready mid-turn) and never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the queued message is consumed → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
